pi_items.source: Task description line 3 specifies 'Table name: sensors'
Task description line 6a specifies 'Type: Text (String)'. String is the valid OGR type name (GDAL documentation). export_result.sh confirms using OGR GetTypeName() which returns 'String'
Task description line 6b specifies 'Type: Integer (Whole Number)'. Integer is a valid OGR/GeoPackage data type per GDAL documentation
Task description line 4 specifies 'Geometry type: Point'. OGR confirms Point is valid geometry type with code 1 (wkbPoint)

Task description line 5 specifies 'EPSG:4326 - WGS 84'. Verified via EPSG.io and spatialreference.org: EPSG:4326 is the standard code for WGS84 geographic coordinate system (latitude/longitude in decimal degrees)
Environment ID is 'qgis_env@0.1' and task uses QGIS for GeoPackage creation. Verified via QGIS documentation: Layer > Create Layer > New GeoPackage Layer menu exists
Verified via OGC official GeoPackage standard: GeoPackage is an open, standards-based SQLite database format for geospatial data (ISO/IEC 12063 and OGC standard)

privileged_info_summary: Task creates a sensor network GeoPackage file at /home/ga/GIS_Data/sensor_network.gpkg in QGIS using Layer > Create Layer > New GeoPackage Layer. The 'sensors' layer uses Point geometry with EPSG:4326 (WGS84) coordinate reference system. Schema includes two fields: model_id (String type) and install_year (Integer type). A single point feature is added with attribute values model_id='EnvSense-X1' and install_year=2024. Data is synthetic (created fresh during task execution with example sensor data). GeoPackage is an OGC-standard SQLite-based geospatial format. All metadata claims are consistent with task description and verified against official documentation.
